task_completion.description: Verify that the 'Sample Order Lines' dataset is open or selected in Oracle Analytics Desktop.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Data panel on the left side of the screen shows 'Sample Order Lines' as the active dataset.

task_completion.description: Verify that a parameter named 'Discount_Rate' has been created with a default value of 10.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A parameter named 'Discount_Rate' is visible in the Parameters pane, or a parameter creation/edit dialog is open showing the name 'Discount_Rate' and the default value '10'.

task_completion.description: Verify that a calculated column named 'Simulated Revenue' has been created.
task_completion.visual_evidence: 'Simulated Revenue' appears in the Data panel (typically under 'My Calculations'), or the calculation editor is open showing the name 'Simulated Revenue' and a formula referencing the parameter.

task_completion.description: Verify that a table visualization has been created containing 'Product Category', 'Sales', and 'Simulated Revenue'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The main canvas displays a table visualization with column headers for 'Product Category', 'Sales', and 'Simulated Revenue'. The Grammar panel should show these data elements assigned to the table.

task_completion.description: Verify that the workbook is saved with the name 'Discount_Simulation'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The top title bar of the Oracle Analytics Desktop window displays 'Discount_Simulation', or a 'Save As' dialog is visible with 'Discount_Simulation' entered as the name.

integrity.description: Verify the agent interacted with the Oracle Analytics Desktop GUI.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the actual Oracle Analytics Desktop application interface being used, rather than terminal commands, scripts, or text editors.

integrity.description: Verify the calculated column is genuinely created using the parameter.
integrity.visual_evidence: The calculation editor shows a formula that mathematically applies the 'Discount_Rate' parameter to 'Sales', rather than just static text or renamed columns.

integrity.description: Verify the table visualization is a native Oracle Analytics Desktop component.
integrity.visual_evidence: The table is rendered within the Oracle Analytics Desktop canvas, and the Grammar/Data panels correctly reflect its configuration, proving it is not a pasted image or external spreadsheet.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The parameter must be named 'Discount_Rate' with a default value of '10'. The calculated column must be named 'Simulated Revenue' and its formula must reference the 'Discount_Rate' parameter. The dataset used must be 'Sample Order Lines'.
